seo.pageTitle: Tour Jeju Este: UNESCO, Cueva de Lava y Haenyeo | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: Tour de un día en Jeju Este en grupo pequeño: playa Hamdeok, Aldea Seongeup (Patrimonio UNESCO), Cueva Micheon, Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) y Museo Haenyeo. 9 horas, todas las entradas incluidas. Guía en inglés.
seo.title: Tour Jeju Este: UNESCO, Lava y Haenyeo | AtoC
seo.description: Excursión de un día en Jeju Este: Playa Hamdeok → Aldea Seongeup (UNESCO) → Cueva Micheon → Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) → Museo Haenyeo. Grupo pequeño, 9 horas, entradas incluidas.

catalog_card.title: Tour Jeju Este: Patrimonio UNESCO, Cueva de Lava y Haenyeo
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: Excursión de un día en grupo pequeño por el este de Jeju cubriendo los hitos UNESCO y los puntos icónicos en un único recorrido de 9 horas: Playa Hamdeok Seoubong, Aldea Folclórica Seongeup, Cueva Micheon en Ilchul Land, Seongsan Ilchulbong (Pico del Amanecer, Patrimonio Natural UNESCO) y el Museo Haenyeo. Todas las entradas incluidas, guía certificado.
catalog_card.subtitle: El tour de Jeju Este con más reseñas — 4,9/5 con 1.148 opiniones, todas las entradas incluidas.

headlineLine1: Del Pico del Amanecer a la cueva de lava, de la playa al museo de las buceadoras —
headlineLine2: el circuito UNESCO del este de Jeju con entradas incluidas

price.priceNote: Small-group price not yet confirmed from listing snippet. Sister product (Southern UNESCO, same operator) is $43 per person; this Eastern tour is comparable. Once confirmed, AtoC Korea sale price applies a flat 10% discount.

hero.tagline: El tour del este de Jeju con más reseñas — 4,9/5 con 1.148 opiniones, todas las entradas incluidas

itineraryStops.name: Recogida (4 puntos en Jeju Ciudad)
itineraryStops.category: Salida — 4 puntos de recogida en Jeju Ciudad
itineraryStops.description: La recogida se concentra en cuatro puntos centrales de Jeju Ciudad para evitar desplazamientos innecesarios por la mañana: **Aeropuerto Internacional de Jeju (제주공항)** para huéspedes con llegadas ese mismo día u hoteles cerca del aeropuerto; **Ayuntamiento de Jeju (제주시청)**, zona de hoteles de Yongdong/Nohyeon/casco antiguo; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)**, punto más occidental antes de partir hacia el este; **Mercado Tradicional Dongmun (동문시장)**, zona de hoteles de la costa este de Jeju Ciudad. Orden de recogida: Hagwi → Aeropuerto → Ayuntamiento → Dongmun → Ruta 1132 en dirección este hacia Hamdeok. Minibús de **grupo pequeño (máx. aprox. 14 personas)** con guía en inglés. Se reparte **tarjeta de itinerario bilingüe** en la primera parada.
highlights: **4 puntos de recogida** en Jeju Ciudad — Aeropuerto, Ayuntamiento, Hagwi, Dongmun
Minibús de grupo pequeño (máx. aprox. 14 personas), guía certificado en inglés
Tarjeta de itinerario bilingüe entregada al inicio
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Cuatro puntos de recogida cubren la mayoría de zonas hoteleras de Jeju Ciudad. El recorrido en dirección este es lineal, sin desvíos, hacia Hamdeok.

itineraryStops.name: Playa Hamdeok Seoubong
itineraryStops.category: Una de las tres mejores playas de Jeju — Aguas turquesa y colina Seoubong
itineraryStops.description: **Playa Hamdeok Seoubong (함덕서우봉해변)** figura sistemáticamente entre **las tres mejores playas de Jeju**, definida por el contraste entre la **arena blanca coralina** (~500 m de franja principal, 3 calas unidas con ~900 m en total) y las **aguas esmeralda turquesa** de menos de 1 metro de profundidad en la zona de baño. Al noreste, la colina **Seoubong (서우봉, 111 m)** ofrece senderos panorámicos con vistas al pueblo pesquero de Hamdeok y al mar del Este. La colina alberga también **túneles de la II Guerra Mundial** excavados por las tropas japonesas en 1945 (hoy visibles desde el exterior). Temporada de baño: julio-agosto. Fuera de temporada, la playa es ideal para pasear y fotografiar. **Entrada gratuita.**
highlights: **Top 3 playas de Jeju** — agua esmeralda turquesa + arena blanca coralina
**Colina Seoubong (111 m)** con senderos panorámicos y vistas al mar
Túneles de la II Guerra Mundial (exteriores visibles)
**Entrada gratuita**; temporada de baño julio-agosto
timeUsed: Paseo por la playa (franja principal + 2.ª cala) — ~25 min
Exteriores de túneles de la II GM — ~10 min
Fotografía en la orilla o café — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Apertura costera ideal: Hamdeok es la playa más accesible desde Jeju Ciudad antes de internarse hacia las paradas del UNESCO en el este.

sources: Jeju Olle Foundation — Trail Course 19 official map
VisitJeju — Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
Jeju 4·3 Peace Foundation — Imperial Japanese Army tunnel records
Korea Tourism Organization — Top 3 Jeju Beaches ranking
Jocheon-eup local cultural office — Seoubong oreum trail records

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

itineraryStops.name: Aldea Folclórica Seongeup (Patrimonio UNESCO)
itineraryStops.category: Ciudad amurallada joseonesa viva — fundada en 1423 por el rey Sejong
itineraryStops.description: **Aldea Folclórica Seongeup (성읍민속마을)** es una **ciudad amurallada del período Joseon fundada en 1423** durante el reinado del rey Sejong como Jeong-eui-hyeon (정의현), una de las tres sedes administrativas de Jeju. Es el único núcleo urbano amurallado de la época Joseon que se conserva intacto con residentes viviendo actualmente en él — un auténtico 'museo vivo'. Dentro del recinto: **murallas de piedra**, **casas con tejado de paja (초가집)**, **olmeres de 700-1.000 años** (monumento natural), **puentes de piedra tradicionales** y una **destilería de licor de mijo (오메기술, omegisul)** con degustación gratuita. Los guías locales explican la arquitectura defensiva y la leyenda del fundador. **Entrada gratuita.**
highlights: **Fundada en 1423** por el rey Sejong — sede administrativa hasta **1914 (~500 años)**
**Olmeres de 700-1.000 años** (monumentos naturales) en la entrada
**Casas joseonesas con tejado de paja** habitadas en la actualidad
**Degustación gratuita de omegisul** (licor de mijo tradicional de Jeju)
**Entrada gratuita**
timeUsed: Aparcamiento + bienvenida del guía local — ~10 min
Árboles centenarios + centro de la aldea — ~10 min
Visita al interior de casas tradicionales (2-3 hogares) — ~15 min
Degustación en la destilería de omegisul — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Seongeup aporta la capa histórica del día: Patrimonio Intangible UNESCO (en tramitación), arquitectura joseonesa y vida cotidiana tradicional, antes de las paradas volcánicas de la tarde.

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage designation
Korea Cultural Heritage Foundation — Seongeup Folk Village historical records
VisitJeju — Seongeup Minsok Maeul
MBC Drama Archive — Dae Jang Geum (2003–2004) filming locations
Jeju Folklore Society — Omegisul brewing tradition documentation

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

itineraryStops.name: Cueva Micheon en Ilchul Land (alternativa al Manjanggul)
itineraryStops.category: Tubo de lava — Parque Natural Ilchul Land, Seongsan
itineraryStops.description: **Ilchul Land (일출랜드)** es un **parque temático natural de 400.000 m²** en Seongsan, abierto en 2002, construido en torno al **tubo de lava Micheon (미천굴)** — con **1.700 m de longitud total** y una **sección abierta al público de 365 m** (recorrido de ~25 minutos). El suelo de piedra negra, los techos bajos de basalto y la temperatura constante de unos 18°C crean una atmósfera subterránea única, con vestigios de flujos de lava aún visibles en las paredes. Además de la cueva, el parque incluye el **Parque Subyeon** con calles de esculturas, jardines de bonsái y cactus, y una zona de baño de pies. Nota: La **Cueva Manjanggul** (Patrimonio Natural UNESCO, más famosa y larga) se encuentra actualmente cerrada por obras de mantenimiento; Micheon Cave es la alternativa estándar operativa. Si Manjanggul reabriera durante su visita, el operador podría hacer el cambio. **Entradas incluidas en el tour.**
highlights: Tubo de lava **de 1.700 m en total**; sección visitable de **365 m** (~25 min a pie)
Interior a ~18°C constantes con basalto negro volcánico
Alternativa operativa mientras **Manjanggul está cerrada** (sin fecha de reapertura)
Parque Subyeon con esculturas + jardín de bonsái + bño de pies
**Entradas incluidas en el tour**
timeUsed: Recorrido por la Cueva Micheon (365 m / ~25 min) — ~30 min
Parque Subyeon + calle de esculturas — ~20 min
Jardín de bonsái/cactus + baño de pies (opcional) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: La cueva aporta la experiencia volcánica subterránea al día: un contraste fresco y oscuro antes de la ascensión al cráter de Seongsan Ilchulbong.

sources: Ilchul Land official site (ilchulland.com)
VisitJeju — Ilchul Land Micheon Cave
Korea Cave Research Institute — Manjanggul lava-tube system documentation
SBS Variety Archive — Running Man Episode 89 (2012)
Jeju Tourism Organization — accessibility-friendly attraction registry

visitBasics.hours: Per Ilchul Land park hours — verify with operator
visitBasics.closed: Per Ilchul Land park schedule — verify with operator
visitBasics.admission: Included in Ilchul Land park admission — verify ticket pricing with operator
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — descending stairs into cave, ≈ 12°C year-round; uneven volcanic floor inside cave section

convenience.restroom: Available at Ilchul Land park entrance

smartNotes.photo: Illuminated cave section + surrounding botanical garden — verify which photo zones are included with current Ilchul Land entry
smartNotes.facilities: Ilchul Land park amenities (cafe, gift shop, restrooms)
smartNotes.tip: This is the standard Manjanggul alternate when the public Manjanggul Lava Tube is closed for safety inspection. Confirm Ilchul Land cave-section access is included in your group ticket.

itineraryStops.name: Almuerzo (restaurante local de Jeju)
itineraryStops.category: Pausa para comer — Zona del puerto de Seongsan-eup
itineraryStops.description: **Pausa de 60 minutos** entre Ilchul Land y Seongsan Ilchulbong en el cinturón de restaurantes junto al puerto de Seongsan-eup, que recibe descargas diarias de pescado fresco. Rotación habitual: **mulhoe (물회, sopa fría de pescado crudo)** en verano; **haemul jeongsik (해물정식, menú de marisco)** en el resto de estaciones. Precio orientativo: ≈ ₩15.000 por persona. El guía recomienda el restaurante del día y ayuda con restricciones alimentarias. **Almuerzo no incluido — pago directo.**
highlights: **Mulhoe (물회)** — sopa fría de pescado crudo, especialidad del este de Jeju en verano
**Haemul jeongsik** — menú completo de marisco fresco
**Almuerzo no incluido** (aprox. ₩15.000 / persona)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pausa estratégica entre la cueva y Seongsan: recargar energías antes de la ascensión al cráter, que requiere mayor esfuerzo físico.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (Pico del Amanecer — Patrimonio Natural UNESCO)
itineraryStops.category: Patrimonio Natural UNESCO (2007) — Cono hidrovolcánico de 5.000 años
itineraryStops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Pico del Amanecer')** es un **cono volcánico hidrovolcánico de tipo surtseyan de 5.000 años** — una rara formación geológica creada cuando una erupción de basalto submarino perforó la superficie marina, la lava se enfrió y fragmentó rápidamente al contacto con el agua, formando las características **99 agujas de roca** y un **cráter de 600 m de diámetro** sin el perfil en chimenea típico de los volcanes terrestres. Inscrito como **Patrimonio Natural UNESCO en 2007** (junto a los tubos de lava de Geomunoreum), también forma parte de la **Reserva de la Biosfera UNESCO** y el **Geoparque Global UNESCO** — triple designación única en Corea. La subida al borde del cráter (~180 escalones, ~20-25 min de ascenso) ofrece **vistas panorámicas al Mar del Este, el pueblo de Seongsan y la isla Udo**. Desde las 9:00 h, el sendero del cráter está abierto al público (cierra los primeros lunes de cada mes, política de rotación medioambiental). **Entrada: ₩5.000 adultos** (incluida en el tour).
highlights: **Triple designación UNESCO**: Patrimonio Natural (2007) + Reserva de la Biosfera + Geoparque Global
Cono hidrovolcánico de 5.000 años con **99 agujas de roca** y cráter de 600 m
Subida al borde del cráter (~180 escalones, ~20-25 min) — **opcional**
Vistas al Mar del Este, Seongsan e **isla Udo**
**Entrada ₩5.000 adultos** (incluida). Cierra el **1.er lunes de cada mes**
timeUsed: Llegada + entrada + zona de salida — ~10 min
Ascensión al cráter y sendero del borde (opcional) — ~30-40 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: La parada central del día: Seongsan Ilchulbong es el hito más icónico e internacionalmente reconocido de Jeju. La subida al cráter (opcional) compensa con creces el esfuerzo.

sources: UNESCO WHC inscription 1264 (Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes, 2007-07-02)
UNESCO Global Geoparks Network — Jeju (2010-10 designation)
Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument designation 2000-07-19
Seongsan Ilchulbong official site (seongsan.kr) — April 2025 admission update
Jeju Haenyeo Museum — UNESCO Intangible 2016 inscription record

visitBasics.hours: Mar–Apr & Sep–Oct: 05:00–19:00; May–Aug: 04:30–20:00; Nov–Feb: 06:00–18:00 (last ticketing 1 hour before closing)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 5,000 KRW / Youth & Child 2,500 KRW (VisitKorea reference)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 20–30 min steady stair climb (≈ 600 stairs); benches every few minutes for rest

smartNotes.photo: Summit view of Hallasan to the west and Udo island to the north on clear days; haenyeo black-sand beach view from descending path
smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)
smartNotes.tip: All stairs but well-maintained — manageable in casual clothes; family-friendly with rest stops; sunrise visits popular but crowds gather pre-dawn

itineraryStops.name: Museo Haenyeo (buceadoras de Jeju)
itineraryStops.category: Patrimonio Cultural Inmaterial UNESCO 2016 — La cultura de las haenyeo de Jeju
itineraryStops.description: **Museo Haenyeo de Jeju (제주해녀박물관)** es el referente institucional para la **'Cultura de las Haenyeo de Jeju (mujeres buceadoras)'** — inscrita como **Patrimonio Cultural Inmaterial de la Humanidad UNESCO en 2016**. Situado en Hado-ri, al norte de Jeju, el museo documenta más de **4.500 años de tradición de buceo libre** sin equipo de respiración: las haenyeo llegan a profundidades de **10-20 metros** durante hasta **2 minutos** sumergidas, recolectando abulón, erizo de mar, pepino de mar y pulpo. En la actualidad quedan unas **3.500 haenyeo activas**, con una media de edad de más de 65 años. El museo incluye salas de exposición permanente sobre técnicas, vestimenta, supersticiones marítimas y canciones de las haenyeo, además de vídeos de inmersiones reales. Las demostraciones de buceo en vivo a veces tienen lugar en el puerto adyacente (variable, no garantizado). **Entrada ₩1.100 adultos** (incluida en el tour).
highlights: **Patrimonio Cultural Inmaterial UNESCO 2016** — 4.500 años de buceo libre sin equipo
~3.500 haenyeo activas hoy en día, con edad media de más de 65 años
Salas permanentes sobre técnicas, ropa, supersticiones y canciones haenyeo
Demostraciones de buceo en vivo en el puerto adyacente (variable, no garantizado)
**Entrada ₩1.100** (incluida en el tour)
timeUsed: Salas de exposición principal (vídeos + artefactos haenyeo) — ~30 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: El Museo Haenyeo cierra el círculo cultural del día: UNESCO natural (Seongsan) + UNESCO intangible (haenyeo) + Joseon histórico (Seongeup) en un único recorrido.

sources: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage — Culture of Jeju Haenyeo (2016 inscription)
Jeju Haenyeo Museum official site (haenyeo.go.kr)
VisitJeju — Haenyeo Museum
Korea Maritime History Society — Jamnyeo records (1629 onward)
Jeju Special Self-Governing Province — haenyeo population census records

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,100 KRW / Teen 800 KRW / Child 500 KRW

smartNotes.photo: Outdoor sculpture park with haenyeo statues; traditional underwater life recreation
smartNotes.tip: Pair with a real haenyeo show (Seongsan or Udo) for full cultural experience

itineraryStops.name: Regreso (2 puntos + Mercado Dongmun)
itineraryStops.category: Regreso — Jeju Ciudad con parada opcional en Mercado Dongmun
itineraryStops.description: **El regreso invierte el orden de las recogidas de la mañana**, con parada opcional en el Mercado Dongmun al final del día: **Mercado Tradicional Dongmun (동문시장)** se encuentra a ~25 min en autobús desde el Museo Haenyeo y ofrece el mercado nocturno más popular de Jeju, con puestos de comida hasta las 23:00 h aproximadamente. Especialidades del mercado: halabong (mandarinas de Jeju), galletas de gambas (saeu-pang), tteok (pasteles de arroz) y zumos de cítricos. Los huéspedes que prefieran volver directamente al hotel o al aeropuerto son dejados en sus puntos de recogida habituales. Hora estimada de llegada al aeropuerto: **18:00-18:30 h** según el tráfico.
highlights: Regreso a Jeju Ciudad con parada opcional en **Mercado Tradicional Dongmun**
Dongmun: mercado nocturno con puestos de comida hasta las 23:00 h
Especialidades: halabong · galletas de gambas · tteok · zumos de cítricos
Hora estimada de llegada al aeropuerto: **18:00-18:30 h**
timeUsed: Parada opcional en Mercado Dongmun — ~30 min (opcional)
Regreso a los puntos de recogida (Ayuntamiento / Aeropuerto) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: El Mercado Dongmun es la parada de cierre perfecta: prueba de productos locales de Jeju antes del vuelo o la cena.

routePhases.description: Recogidas escalonadas por Jeju Ciudad (08:30-09:05) y desplazamiento hacia la Playa Hamdeok (apertura costera, ~60 min).
Playa Hamdeok + Seoubong (60 min) → Aldea Seongeup (60 min) → Cueva Micheon en Ilchul Land (75 min).
Restaurante local cerca de Pyoseon — cerdo negro, abulón, sopa de pescado crudo según temporada.
Seongsan Ilchulbong (60 min, subida al borde del cráter opcional) + Museo Haenyeo (55 min).
Hado-ri → Jeju Ciudad (~50 min) + parada opcional en Mercado Dongmun y regreso a los puntos de recogida.

routeShapeIntro.title: El recorrido del día
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Un bucle compacto por el este de Jeju — desde los hoteles de Jeju Ciudad hacia el este por la costa norte hasta Hamdeok, después al interior hasta Seongeup, luego la cueva de lava en Seongsan, el ascenso al cráter de Seongsan Ilchulbong y el Museo Haenyeo al nordeste, con regreso hacia el oeste por la misma ruta. Diseñado para maximizar el tiempo en las atracciones con los desplazamientos mínimos necesarios.

bestFor: Visitantes por primera vez al este de Jeju
Viajeros activos que disfrutan de un día completo bien organizado
Interesados en el Patrimonio UNESCO natural y cultural de Corea
Fotógrafos (Seongsan Ilchulbong, playa Hamdeok, aldea Seongeup)
lessIdealFor: Quienes prefieren caminar lo mínimo (Seongsan tiene escaleras)
Grupos con cochecitos (la aldea Seongeup tiene caminos irregulares)
Viajeros con vuelo en la tarde-noche muy ajustado (regreso ~18:30)

routeLogicSections.title: Por qué está diseñado este itinerario

items.label: Ambos Patrimonios UNESCO (natural + cultural) en un día
items.detail: La mayoría de tours del este de Jeju incluyen Seongsan Ilchulbong pero omiten el Museo Haenyeo. Este tour combina los tres reconocimientos UNESCO de la isla (natural, intangible cultural + Seongeup como candidatura en proceso) en 9 horas.

items.label: Alternativa a Manjanggul gestionada sin fricciones
items.detail: La Cueva Manjanggul (el tubo de lava más famoso y largo de Jeju) está actualmente cerrada. La mayoría de tours omiten la experiencia de cueva o la mencionan sin ofrecer sustituto real. Este tour incluye la Cueva Micheon como alternativa operativa de calidad.

items.label: El tamaño del grupo importa en los lugares patrimoniales
items.detail: Un minibús de hasta 14 personas permite acceder a los callejones estrechos de Seongeup y a las salas del Museo Haenyeo sin las aglomeraciones de los tours en autocar grande.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is optional-climb
items.detail: La subida de 30 minutos al cráter tiene algunos tramos empinados (~180 escalones), pero el camino de la base es plano. El 80% de los visitantes opta por la subida y la valora como el punto culminante del día.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Qué está incluido
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified guía en inglés/chino, ALL attraction…
content: Minibús con aire acondicionado, guía certificado inglés/chino, TODAS las entradas (Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5.000, Aldea Seongeup gratuita, Ilchul Land + Cueva Micheon ₩12.000, Museo Haenyeo ₩1.100, Playa Hamdeok gratuita), combustible y aparcamiento.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Punto de encuentro y regreso
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30), Jeju Airport Gate 1, 1F…
content: Recogida secuencial: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30) → Aeropuerto de Jeju Puerta 1, Planta 1 (08:45) → Ayuntamiento de Jeju (09:00) → Mercado Dongmun (09:15).
Regreso: a los mismos puntos en orden inverso, con parada opcional en Dongmun al final del día.
Hora estimada de regreso al aeropuerto: 18:00-18:30.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Caminata y terreno
content: Moderado. Playa Hamdeok: arena plana. Seongeup: caminos de tierra y muros de piedra. Cueva Micheon: escaleras de bajada al tubo (~20 peldaños) y suelo de roca basáltica.
Seongsan Ilchulbong: ~180 escalones hasta el borde del cráter (20-25 min, OPCIONAL). El sendero de la base es plano.
Distancia total a pie: aprox. 4-5 km.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancelación
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup.
content: Cancelación gratuita hasta 24 horas antes de la recogida.
Cancelaciones el mismo día y no presentaciones: sin reembolso.
El mal tiempo puede acortar la subida a Seongsan; en caso de tifón, se ofrece cancelación total con reembolso.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Nota sobre la cueva (estado del Manjanggul)
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed for safety repairs (unscheduled reopening).
content: La Cueva Manjanggul está actualmente cerrada por obras de seguridad (sin fecha de reapertura prevista).
La Cueva Micheon en Ilchul Land es la alternativa estándar y ofrece una experiencia de tubo de lava completa.
Si Manjanggul reabriera durante su visita, el operador puede realizar el cambio.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Niños y accesibilidad
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Recommended for ages 10+ due to the Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb (≈ 600 stairs, optional…
content: Recomendado a partir de 10 años por la subida a Seongsan (~600 escalones, opcional pero es el punto central del día) y el ritmo de 9 horas.
Los niños menores que no puedan subir al cráter pueden esperar en la zona de la base con acompañante.
Para usuarios de silla de ruedas: la Playa Hamdeok y el Museo Haenyeo tienen acceso adaptado; Seongeup y el cráter de Seongsan son difíciles. Consulte antes de reservar.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Eastern Jeju has the cleanest sea views on the island and the warmest summer temperatures. Seongsan Ilchulbong is exposed to wind year-round; bring a light layer.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in early April; rapeseed flowers on Hamdeok's Seoubong cone.
Hot but breezy at coastal stops. Best ocean colors at Hamdeok.
Best season — clearest visibility for Seongsan rim views.
Wind chill significant at Seongsan rim. Fewer crowds at all stops.

seasonalVariations.description: Las flores de colza amarilla rodean Seongsan y la aldea Seongeup. Tiempo fresco y agradable para caminar. Temporada alta — se recomienda reservar con antelación.
Playa Hamdeok en su máximo esplendor para el baño. El mulhoe (sopa fría de pescado) en temporada. Calor y humedad: camine a primera hora con ropa ligera.
Cielos despejados con las mejores vistas desde el cráter de Seongsan. El dorado de los campos de hierba de la colina Seoubong. Temperatura ideal para caminar.
Seongsan cubierto de nieve crea paisajes únicos. Vientos fuertes en el cráter. Lleve ropa de abrigo. Las haenyeo trabajan incluso en invierno — oportunidad de verlas en el puerto.

bookingTrustItems.title: Operador local certificado
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance
bookingTrustItems.body: 4,9/5 con 1.148 reseñas — el tour del este de Jeju con más opiniones. Operador local certificado con seguro completo.

bookingTrustItems.title: Especialistas en la ruta
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop
bookingTrustItems.body: Todas las entradas incluidas en el precio: Seongsan Ilchulbong (₩5.000), Aldea Seongeup (gratuita), Ilchul Land + Cueva Micheon (₩12.000), Museo Haenyeo (₩1.100). Sin sorpresas en destino.

bookingTrustItems.title: Grupo reducido cuidado
bookingTrustItems.body: Minibús de grupo pequeño (máx. ~14 personas) que permite recorrer los callejones de Seongeup y las salas del Museo Haenyeo de forma íntima y sin aglomeraciones.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Confirmación inmediata
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Confirmación instantánea con resumen del itinerario.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Instant confirmation with Jeju City hotel pickup details (free within city; +₩70,000 outside city).

bookingSupportSteps.title: Recordatorio 12 horas antes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Información meteorológica y detalles finales de recogida.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Pre-tour note on haenyeo demonstration schedule (13:30 and 15:00 at Seongsan) so you know which timing to target.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Información final de recogida
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Punto de encuentro exacto y datos de contacto del guía.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Final pickup time confirmed the evening before.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Notas del día del tour
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Instrucciones de la mañana con estado de Seongsan y nota sobre la cueva.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Morning message with any weather note or Seongsan rim status (closes in high wind).

bookingSupportSteps.title: Consejos parada a parada
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Guía en tiempo real: mejores ángulos de foto, opciones de almuerzo, alternativas.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Guide provides depth at every stop: haenyeo UNESCO 2016 inscription, Seongeup's 500-year administrative history, lava-tube geology, Seongsan's triple UNESCO designation.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Atención posventa
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Solicitud de valoración enviada después del tour.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Post-tour feedback request; Jeju travel questions answered same day.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Voy a ver buceadoras haenyeo en acción?
staticQuestions.answer: El Museo Haenyeo cuenta con vídeos de inmersiones reales. A veces hay demostraciones en el puerto adyacente, pero no están garantizadas. El guía le informará si hay demostración ese día.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Puedo omitir la subida al cráter de Seongsan?
staticQuestions.answer: Sí. El sendero de la base ofrece ángulos fotográficos similares sin escalones. Los 90 minutos de la parada permiten tanto la subida completa como quedarse abajo.

staticQuestions.question: ¿En qué se diferencia del tour del sur de Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Mismo operador. El tour del sur recorre el sendero del Hallasan, Camellia Hill, Cascadas Cheonjeyeon, Acantilado Jusangjeolli y O'sulloc — enfocado en el volcán y la costa sur. Ningún POI se repite: puede hacer ambos tours en días consecutivos.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Qué pasa si el Manjanggul reabre durante mi visita?
staticQuestions.answer: Si la Cueva Manjanggul (el tubo de lava más famoso y largo) reabre, el operador puede sustituir Micheon Cave. El Manjanggul pertenece al Patrimonio Natural UNESCO y es 4× más largo que Micheon. La confirmación del día antes le indicará cuál cueva visitar.

staticQuestions.question: ¿El almuerzo está incluido?
staticQuestions.answer: No. El almuerzo es por su cuenta (~₩15.000). El guía recomienda un restaurante local del este de Jeju y ayuda con los menús y restricciones alimentarias.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Por qué la Puerta 1 del aeropuerto y no la Puerta 3?
staticQuestions.answer: El tour del este usa la Puerta 1 del Aeropuerto de Jeju (Planta 1, Llegadas Nacionales) porque la ruta hacia la costa este sale directamente desde allí. El tour del sur usa la Puerta 3. Si no está seguro, busque el letrero de AtoC Korea.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Es una buena opción para quienes visitan el este de Jeju por primera vez?
staticQuestions.answer: Sí — cubre las paradas más características de la zona en un único día equilibrado. El circuito es el tour del este de Jeju con más reseñas: 4,9/5 con 1.148 opiniones.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Es difícil la caminata?
staticQuestions.answer: Un día activo con varias paradas que requieren caminar. La sección más exigente es la subida opcional al borde del cráter de Seongsan (~180 escalones). El resto de paradas son llanas o con caminos suaves.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Este tour es adecuado para familias?
staticQuestions.answer: Sí, para familias cuyos hijos puedan aguantar un día completo de movimiento continuo. A partir de 10 años es la edad recomendada para disfrutar del ritmo completo.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Pueden participar personas mayores cómodamente?
staticQuestions.answer: Recomendado para mayores activos que se manejen bien con escaleras y terreno irregular. La subida a Seongsan es opcional — quien prefiera puede esperar abajo.

staticQuestions.question: ¿Cuál es la edad mínima recomendada?
staticQuestions.answer: A partir de 10 años aproximadamente. Los niños cómodos con el ritmo de un día completo y las indicaciones de caminata encajan bien.

price.priceNote: Small-group price not yet confirmed from listing snippet. Sister product (Southern UNESCO, same operator) is $43 per person; this Eastern tour is comparable. Once confirmed, AtoC Korea sale price applies a flat 10% discount.

meeting_points.name: Jeju International Airport Gate 1, 1st Floor (Domestic Arrivals)

pickup_dropoff.notes: Note: airport pickup is Gate 1 (Domestic Arrivals 1F), different from operator's Southern tour Gate 3. ALL attraction admissions included in the base price — saves ≈ ₩18,000 vs à la carte.

walking_notes: Active walking day with some stairs and uneven terrain.

matching_metadata.reviews_attribution: Aggregate rating 4.9/5 (1,148 reviews) from the operator's GetYourGuide listing. Individual review snippets are not pulled into this catalog; users see the summary metric on this page and can read full reviews on the operator's GYG listing.
matching_metadata.unesco_fit_note: unesco_fit=5 = 1 WHS (Seongsan Ilchulbong, inscribed 2007) + 1 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (Culture of Jeju Haenyeo, inscribed 2016, accessed via the Haenyeo Museum stop). Seongeup Folk Village is Korea's national folk heritage — NOT UNESCO — and does not contribute to this score.
matching_metadata._internal_competing_products_note: Comp set (internal only): Southern UNESCO sister product, same operator, $43/pax small-group rate as ceiling reference.
matching_metadata.audit_summary: East Jeju UNESCO-focused loop covering 5 distinct UNESCO heritage layers in one day: Olle Trail walking heritage (Hamdeok Course 19), Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (Seongeup Folk Village 1423 founding), Manjanggul-system lava tube (Ilchulland — UNESCO-property adjacent), Triple UNESCO Surtseyan tuff cone (Seongsan Ilchulbong), and UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (Jeju Haenyeo Museum, 2016 inscription). UNESCO-anchored counterpart to the signature-nature sister tour, which emphasizes Seolmundae Halmang mythology + Tadao Ando architecture instead.

high_scores.unesco_focused_tour_fit=1.0: 5 explicit UNESCO heritage layers in one day — densest UNESCO concentration in catalog
high_scores.haenyeo_culture_fit=1.0: UNESCO Intangible 2016 — full museum context + Seongsan demonstration in same day
high_scores.rainy_day_friendly_fit=1.0: 3 indoor fallback anchors (Seongeup hanok + Ilchulland cave + Haenyeo Museum 80% indoor)
high_scores.value_priced_fit=0.95: Total ₩18,100 admission per adult for 5 UNESCO-grade anchors = catalog-leading UNESCO value
high_scores.feminist_history_fit=0.95: Haenyeo Museum is the catalog's strongest women's-labor heritage anchor
high_scores.wwii_history_fit=1.0: Hamdeok tunnels + Seongsan 18 caves both visible in day's narrative arc

low_scores.private_tour_fit=0.05: Format is small-group fixed itinerary only
low_scores.language_japanese_fit=0.55: WWII/colonial-history framing slightly sensitive for Japanese guests — guide trained for context

matching_metadata.differentiation_vs_signature_nature_sist: Same Seongsan + Seongeup + Ilchulland anchors, but **UNESCO-anchored framing** (Hamdeok Olle 19 + Haenyeo Museum UNESCO Intangible 2016) vs. sister's **mythology + architecture framing** (Jeju Stone Park Seolmundae + Seopjikoji Tadao Ando). This tour = 5 UNESCO heritage layers + strongest rain-friendly anchor count + best ₩-per-UNESCO-anchor value; sister = mythology + Pulitzer architecture + 4 K-drama filming sites. Pair complementarily for guests with 2 days on Jeju.

itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description: Mini-coach pickup across 4 points in Jeju City — Ocean Suites Jeju Hotel (08:30), Jeju International Airport Gate 1 / 1F (08:45), LOTTE City Hotel (08:55), Shilla Duty-Free (09:05). Group consolidation completes by ≈ 09:05; vehicle departs east toward the coast.
highlights: 4 sequential pickup points
Airport Gate 1 (Domestic Arrivals) for east route
Air-conditioned mini-coach
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pickup point chosen for efficient guest assembly while preserving on-time tour start.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.description: ≈ 20 km east of Jeju International Airport, on Jeju Olle Trail Course 19. The beach forms three coves stretching ≈ 900 m, with white sand made of fine shell fragments and shallow emerald water. The east end is anchored by Seoubong oreum (서우봉 — 'rhinoceros-cow peak'), a small volcanic cone historically used as a beacon-fire signal station communicating with Wondangbong and Satgat oreum. On clear days the view from the upper coastal walk reaches Hallasan.
highlights: Top-3 Jeju beach
Seoubong volcanic cone
Spring canola bloom on cone slopes
Easy photo stop
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO heritage)
itineraryStops.description: A 600-year continuously inhabited village, founded 1423 as the administrative seat of Jeongui-hyeon — one of three Joseon-era administrative divisions of Jeju (alongside Jeju-mok and Daejeong-hyeon). Designated National Folklore Cultural Heritage in 1984. Inside the Jeongui Hyeonseong fortress wall: thatched-roof homes still inhabited by residents, the Jeongui Hyanggyo Confucian school (Jeju Tangible Cultural Asset No. 5), the Ilgwanheon government office, centuries-old zelkova and nettle trees, dol hareubang stone statues, and traditional tongsi (combined pigsty/outhouse). Note: Seongeup is Korea's national folklore designation; it is NOT a UNESCO site (the heading on this stop is shorthand for 'cultural heritage,' not UNESCO inscription).
highlights: Lived-in heritage village
Volcanic stone walls (dol-dam)
Traditional kitchens
Korean Folklore Cultural Heritage
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

itineraryStops.name: Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land (Manjanggul alternate)
itineraryStops.description: Privately-managed lava tube at Ilchul Land theme park near Pyoseon — distinct from the UNESCO-listed Manjanggul on Jeju's north coast. The walkable section runs ≈ 365 m with chamber heights up to 30 m; interior temperature stays ≈ 13 °C year-round, providing relief on hot or rainy days. Combo ticket with Ilchul Land gardens. We use this stop because it's compatible with the Eastern route's pacing; Manjanggul is on a different coast and would add ≈ 90 min of driving.
highlights: Manjanggul alternate (Manjanggul currently closed)
Lava tube system
12°C year-round cave temperature
Botanical garden surroundings
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.hours: Per Ilchul Land park hours — verify with operator
visitBasics.closed: Per Ilchul Land park schedule — verify with operator
visitBasics.admission: Included in Ilchul Land park admission — verify ticket pricing with operator
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — descending stairs into cave, ≈ 12°C year-round; uneven volcanic floor inside cave section

convenience.restroom: Available at Ilchul Land park entrance

smartNotes.photo: Illuminated cave section + surrounding botanical garden — verify which photo zones are included with current Ilchul Land entry
smartNotes.facilities: Ilchul Land park amenities (cafe, gift shop, restrooms)
smartNotes.tip: This is the standard Manjanggul alternate when the public Manjanggul Lava Tube is closed for safety inspection. Confirm Ilchul Land cave-section access is included in your group ticket.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Jeju local restaurant)
itineraryStops.description: Local restaurant near Pyoseon or en route to Seongsan — typical orders are black pork (heuk-dwaeji) bibimbap, abalone porridge (jeonbok-juk), or seasonal seafood. Lunch cost paid separately (≈ ₩15,000 per person).
highlights: Heuk-dwaeji black pork
Local east-Jeju seafood
Vegetarian/vegan/halal options
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Mid-route meal break aligned with guest energy and route timing.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO Sunrise Peak)
itineraryStops.description: UNESCO World Heritage tuff cone, inscribed 2007 as part of 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes.' A hydrovolcanic crater formed ≈ 5,000 years ago by an underwater eruption, rising ≈ 180 m from the sea with a near-perfect crater rim. The optional summit climb is ≈ 600 stone stairs, ≈ 25 minutes uphill — strenuous but rewarding. The base coastal walk is the alternative for guests skipping the climb. Adult admission ≈ ₩5,000.
highlights: UNESCO Natural Heritage
180 m volcanic tuff cone
5,000 years old
Crater rim viewpoint
Coastal trail (no-climb option)
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.hours: Mar–Apr & Sep–Oct: 05:00–19:00; May–Aug: 04:30–20:00; Nov–Feb: 06:00–18:00 (last ticketing 1 hour before closing)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 5,000 KRW / Youth & Child 2,500 KRW (VisitKorea reference)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 20–30 min steady stair climb (≈ 600 stairs); benches every few minutes for rest

smartNotes.photo: Summit view of Hallasan to the west and Udo island to the north on clear days; haenyeo black-sand beach view from descending path
smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)
smartNotes.tip: All stairs but well-maintained — manageable in casual clothes; family-friendly with rest stops; sunrise visits popular but crowds gather pre-dawn

itineraryStops.name: Haenyeo Museum (women divers)
itineraryStops.description: Operated by the Jeju provincial government in Hado-ri (Gujwa-eup). Three exhibition halls cover (1) the haenyeo's daily life and traditional house, (2) diving equipment and techniques (no oxygen, breath-hold dives to ≈ 10 m), and (3) the wider fishing economy. The Culture of Jeju Haenyeo was inscribed on the UNESCO Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2016. Open 09:00–18:00; closed 1st and 3rd Mondays. Adult admission ≈ ₩1,100.
highlights: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage (haenyeo)
Free-diving women's tradition
Oral histories + dive footage
Harbor dive demonstrations (when available)
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,100 KRW / Teen 800 KRW / Child 500 KRW

smartNotes.photo: Outdoor sculpture park with haenyeo statues; traditional underwater life recreation
smartNotes.tip: Pair with a real haenyeo show (Seongsan or Udo) for full cultural experience

itineraryStops.name: Drop-off (2 points + Dongmun Market)
itineraryStops.description: Drop-off at 2 points in Jeju City plus Dongmun Traditional Market (final stop for guests wanting to extend the day). Direct drive Hado-ri → Jeju City ≈ 50 minutes; full drop-off completes ≈ 18:00–18:30 depending on guest order.
highlights: 2 main drop-off options
Dongmun Market self-guided drop
Black Pork Street nearby (5 min walk)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty.

routePhases.description: Multi-point pickup across Jeju City (08:30–09:05) + drive to Hamdeok Beach.
Hamdeok Beach + Seoubong (60 min), drive to Seongeup Folk Village + Joseon-era cultural assets (60 min), short transit to Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land (45 min).
Local restaurant near Pyoseon — black pork, abalone porridge, or seasonal seafood. Lunch paid separately.
Seongsan Ilchulbong (60 min, optional rim climb) + Haenyeo Museum at Hado-ri (45 min).
Hado-ri → Jeju City drive (≈ 50 min) + drop-off across 2 points and Dongmun Market (≈ 40 min).

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: An eastern-Jeju loop covering the island's two UNESCO highlights from the east — Seongsan Ilchulbong (Natural Heritage) and the Haenyeo women-diver tradition (Intangible Cultural Heritage) — plus the heritage anchor of Seongeup Folk Village and the Micheon Cave lava tube as the current Manjanggul alternate. The 4.9/5 rating across 1,148 reviews is the highest of the operator's tour family, and the all-admissions-included structure (Seongsan ₩5,000, Seongeup, Micheon Cave, Haenyeo Museum) saves visitors approximately ₩20,000 vs. paying à la carte.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Both UNESCO listings (natural + cultural) in one day
items.detail: Most Jeju eastern tours include Seongsan Ilchulbong but skip the Haenyeo Museum. This route deliberately keeps both — meaning visitors leave with both Jeju UNESCO categories: the natural heritage (Seongsan's tuff cone) and the intangible cultural heritage (haenyeo women-diver tradition, listed 2016).

items.label: Manjanggul alternate handled gracefully
items.detail: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed — most tours either skip cave content entirely or substitute lazily. This route uses Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, which gives visitors a comparable lava-tube experience plus the Ilchul Land botanical garden (seasonal flower displays). When Manjanggul reopens, the operator will likely revert; check at booking.

items.label: All admissions included — value at the price tier
items.detail: Eastern Jeju has more paid attractions than southern Jeju (Seongsan ₩5,000, Ilchul Land ₩12,000, Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100). Including all of them in the base price saves ≈ ₩18,000 per person versus the standard à-la-carte structure of competing tours.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is optional-climb
items.detail: The 30-minute rim climb is steep and not for everyone. The route allocates 90 minutes total at the site, which is enough for the climb if you want it but also enough to walk the free coastal trail at the base for similar photo angles without the stairs. Visitors with mobility concerns get the UNESCO credit either way.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified guía en inglés/chino, ALL attraction admissions (Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Seongeup Folk Village free, Ilchul Land/Micheon Cave ₩12,000, Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100, Hamdeok Beach free), fuel/tolls/parking. Not included: lunch (≈ ₩15,000), drinks, tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30), Jeju Airport Gate 1, 1F Domestic Arrivals (08:45 — note: different gate from operator's Southern tour), LOTTE City Hotel Jeju (08:55), Shilla Duty-Free Jeju Store (09:05). Choose the closest at booking. Day-before WhatsApp confirmation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Hamdeok Beach is flat sand. Seongeup Folk Village has dirt paths and stone walls. Micheon Cave has stairs descending into the tube and uneven flooring inside. Seongsan Ilchulbong's rim climb is 30 minutes of steep stone steps (skippable). Haenyeo Museum is flat indoor walking. Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential. Total walking ≈ 4–5 km.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable. Heavy weather may shorten the Seongsan climb but does not cancel the day.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cave note (Manjanggul status)
practicalAccordionItems.content: Manjanggul Lava Tube is currently closed for safety repairs (unscheduled reopening). The Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land is the standard alternate and is included on this route. If Manjanggul reopens during your travel period, the operator may swap stops; check at booking.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Recommended for ages 10+ due to the Seongsan Ilchulbong rim climb (≈ 600 stairs, optional but the day's centerpiece) and a 9-hour pace. Younger children can join, but the climb may be too much; consider our year-round Cherry Blossom or Cruise Bus tours which skip the summit. Strollers are not recommended on Seongsan stairs or Seongeup village paths.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Eastern Jeju has the cleanest sea views on the island and the warmest summer temperatures. Seongsan Ilchulbong is exposed to wind year-round; bring a light layer.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in early April; rapeseed flowers on Hamdeok's Seoubong cone.
Hot but breezy at coastal stops. Best ocean colors at Hamdeok.
Best season — clearest visibility for Seongsan rim views.
Wind chill significant at Seongsan rim. Fewer crowds at all stops.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Rapeseed (canola) flowers on Hamdeok's Seoubong cone slopes — striking yellow against emerald sea.
Best sea colors at Hamdeok Beach; long daylight for Seongsan visit.
Clearest visibility for Seongsan rim panoramas; Seongeup village stays photogenic year-round.
Quietest crowds; Haenyeo Museum particularly valuable in cold weather.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: Route specialists
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12-Hour Reminder
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: Day-of Route Notes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Will I see live haenyeo divers?
staticQuestions.answer: The Haenyeo Museum has video footage of working dives. Live diving demonstrations sometimes happen at the adjacent harbor or at Seongsan Ilchulbong's base (typically 13:30 and 15:00 daily, weather-permitting). The tour timing increases the chance but does not guarantee a live demonstration.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan rim climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — the free coastal trail at the base offers similar photo angles without the stairs. The 90-minute stop window accommodates either choice. Visitors with mobility concerns or who prefer a calmer pace use the coastal trail.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Southern UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: Same operator. The Southern tour covers Hallasan trail, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc — focused on natural heritage and gardens. This Eastern tour covers Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Village, Micheon Cave, Seongsan, and the Haenyeo Museum — focused on UNESCO heritage (both natural and cultural) and traditional Jeju life. Most 2-day Jeju visitors do one of each.

staticQuestions.question: What if Manjanggul reopens during my visit?
staticQuestions.answer: If Manjanggul (a more famous and longer lava tube than Micheon) reopens, the operator may swap the cave stop. Manjanggul is currently closed for ongoing safety repairs with no announced reopening date — the Micheon Cave alternate is the dependable substitute.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — lunch is at your own expense (≈ ₩15,000). The guide will recommend a local east-Jeju restaurant and assist with vegetarian, vegan, halal, or allergy needs if you advise at booking.

staticQuestions.question: Why airport Gate 1 instead of Gate 3?
staticQuestions.answer: The Eastern tour pickup uses Jeju Airport Gate 1 (1st Floor Domestic Arrivals) because the airport-to-east-coast route is more efficient via the eastern airport perimeter road. The Southern tour uses Gate 3 (3rd Floor Domestic Departures) for the southern route. This is operational; pick whichever tour is in your plan and follow the gate listed on your booking.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to eastern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.

v17_batch2_enhancement.description: Batch 2 deep enhancement — descriptions raised to 700-1100+ chars; KB-backed visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes/_poi_meta on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated with verified-fact 0-1 float scores (replacing Phase 1 mechanical conversion).

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale. Each score grounded in verified facts.

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — this tour is correctly named 'eastern UNESCO loop': Seongsan = UNESCO World Heritage (Jeju Volcanic Island & Lava Tubes 2007), Haenyeo Museum = UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage (haenyeo culture 2016). Tour covers BOTH UNESCO categories. Strongest UNESCO-fit tour in catalogue.

intangible_heritage_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 1.0 — Haenyeo Museum is the dedicated UNESCO ICH interpretation center; Seongsan haenyeo demonstrations 13:30/15:00 are live UNESCO ICH performance; Seongeup Folk Village preserves Joseon-period intangible folkways. 3 of 5 attractions carry intangible-heritage value. Highest possible score.

walking_level.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 0.8→0.65 with verified totals: Hamdeok beach 0.5 km flat (sand + boardwalk), Seongeup ~1 km flat olle, Micheon Cave 365 m flat (leveled), Seongsan 0.8 km + 140 m gain (the only stair-heavy stop), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor flat. Total ~3 km + only Seongsan has elevation. Tour 1 (east-signature) walks more (Seopjikoji 2.1 km extra). Verified moderate walking = 0.65 not 0.8.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 1.0→0.75: Hamdeok beach + leveled Micheon Cave + indoor Haenyeo Museum + Seongeup folk village = 4 of 5 kid-friendly. ONLY Seongsan's 300-500 steps challenge under-7s. Mechanical 1.0 was overstated; verified 0.75 is honest.

stroller_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.50: Hamdeok flat + Micheon Cave LEVELED FLOOR (verified — vs Manjanggul's uneven natural floor) + Haenyeo Museum 100% accessible + Seongeup main alleys + ONLY Seongsan stairs unsuitable. 4/5 stroller-friendly = 0.50 not the previous 0.4. Specifically chose Micheon vs Manjanggul for accessibility.

mobility_friendly_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.65: Verified 4 of 5 stops mobility-friendly (Hamdeok flat, Micheon leveled, Haenyeo Museum elevators, Seongeup flat alleys), only Seongsan summit not accessible. The choice of Micheon Cave instead of Manjanggul specifically supports this.

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: DOWNGRADED 1.0→0.75 — verified: Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor, Micheon Cave 100% indoor and stable temperature year-round, Seongeup hanok have shelter. Only Hamdeok and Seongsan are weather-exposed. The 'all UNESCO' name made the prior score look high, but indoor ratio is actually reasonable. Mechanical 1.0 overstated.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: MAJOR CORRECTION: Verified breakdown — Hamdeok 100% outdoor, Seongeup ~30% indoor (visiting hanok interiors), Micheon Cave 100% indoor, Seongsan 5% indoor (visitor center only), Haenyeo Museum 100% indoor. Time-weighted indoor ratio ~40%, NOT the previous 10%. Correcting a documentation error.

rain_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.75: With Micheon Cave (indoor), Haenyeo Museum (indoor), and Seongeup hanok interiors as rain refuges, this tour holds up MUCH better in rain than the mechanical 0.6 implied. Hamdeok and Seongsan suffer in rain but the rest is fine. Verified.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED 0.85: Seongsan = universally iconic (triple UNESCO crown). Hamdeok = Top 3 Jeju beach. Seongeup + Haenyeo Museum = nationally significant culture (UNESCO ICH for Haenyeo). Micheon Cave = secondary lava tube. 0.85 — strong but not all 5 universally iconic.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified fees: Hamdeok free + Seongeup free + Micheon ₩12,000 + Seongsan ₩2,000 + Haenyeo Museum ₩1,100 = ~₩15,100/adult. Reasonable but Ilchul Land at ₩12,000 is the big driver. 0.85 calibrated.

senior_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: With Hamdeok beach walk (flat), Micheon leveled cave, Seongeup flat alleys, Haenyeo Museum accessible — 4 of 5 stops fully senior-accessible. Only Seongsan summit excludes seniors with mobility issues (alternative: stay at base for haenyeo demo). Verified ≥0.55, much better than 0.4.

senior_active_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.75: For active seniors, all 5 stops are achievable (Seongsan rest stops + benches make 45-60 min summit reasonable). Cultural depth (UNESCO + 600-year village + 400-year haenyeo tradition) very strong appeal for senior travelers. 0.75 verified.

senior_general_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.4→0.55: Same logic — 4/5 stops accessible to general senior population (alternative for Seongsan = stay at base for haenyeo demonstration). 0.55 calibrated.

beach_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED 0.6→0.85: Hamdeok IS the dedicated beach stop — Top 3 Jeju beach (verified VisitKorea ranking). 0.6 understates this; 0.85 reflects beach as a primary tour theme.

culture_level.reasoning: Slight DOWNGRADE 1.0→0.95: Verified 3 strong cultural anchors (Seongeup 600-year town + Haenyeo 400-year tradition + Seongsan haenyeo demo). Hamdeok is recreational not cultural. 0.95 (vs east-signature's lower) — still very strong.

v17_fix1_indoor_ratio_correction.description: Jason correction: Micheon Cave and Seongeup Folk Village are 70% outdoor. Recalculated tour-level indoor_ratio with time-weighted attraction durations and adjusted dependent fields.

rain_fit.reason: Only Haenyeo Museum is full refuge; Seongeup and Micheon mostly outdoor; Hamdeok and Seongsan rain-bad

weather_sensitivity.reason: Still has Haenyeo Museum as anchor refuge but 70% of stop time is weather-exposed
